description:
You are an “artifact-manager” responsible for curating three on-prem binary repositories.
    A recent outage requires that you document – in a reproducible, machine-parsable way – whether
    the repository front-end services are listening on their expected TCP port (8081).

    1. Repository inventory  
       ─────────────────────  
       repo-alpha.internal  : 8081  
       repo-beta.internal   : 8081  
       repo-gamma.internal  : 8081  

    2. Diagnostic requirements  
       a. The check **must be executed concurrently** (i.e. the three TCP connection attempts
          run in parallel, not sequentially). Use any standard userspace tool available in a
          typical Linux distribution (for example: `nc`, `timeout`, `bash` job control, etc.).
       b. Each individual probe MUST abort after **3 seconds** if no connection is established.
       c. A probe result is interpreted as follows  
          • CONNECT succeeds → “OPEN”  
          • CONNECT times-out or is refused → “UNREACHABLE”

    3. Artefacts that must exist when you are finished  
       a. Directory: /home/user/diagnostics  
          (create it if it does not yet exist)  
       b. Main log file: /home/user/diagnostics/repo_connectivity_01.log  
          • Exactly one line per repository, in the order shown in section 1.  
          • Strict line format (**no leading/trailing spaces**):  
              HOSTNAME|PORT|STATUS  
          • Example of a single line (do not copy; follow real data):  
              repo-alpha.internal|8081|UNREACHABLE  
       c. Summary file: /home/user/diagnostics/summary.txt  
          • One single line, no newline before/after:  
              Total repositories checked: <N> | Successful: <S> | Unreachable: <U>

    4. Expected numeric values for the summary  
       N = 3 (because there are three repositories)  
       S = number of lines whose STATUS is “OPEN”  
       U = number of lines whose STATUS is “UNREACHABLE”

    5. Verification hints (for you)  
       • Make sure the main log’s lines are *exactly* in the sequence:  
         repo-alpha.internal, repo-beta.internal, repo-gamma.internal.  
       • STATUS must be upper-case ASCII.  
       • The automated grader will read both files verbatim; therefore
         pay close attention to delimiters (vertical bar “|”) and spelling.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data used by the grader – DO NOT LEAK! -->
    <expected_directory>/home/user/diagnostics</expected_directory>

    <expected_main_log>/home/user/diagnostics/repo_connectivity_01.log</expected_main_log>
    <expected_main_log_content>
repo-alpha.internal|8081|UNREACHABLE
repo-beta.internal|8081|UNREACHABLE
repo-gamma.internal|8081|UNREACHABLE
    </expected_main_log_content>

    <expected_summary_file>/home/user/diagnostics/summary.txt</expected_summary_file>
    <expected_summary_content>Total repositories checked: 3 | Successful: 0 | Unreachable: 3</expected_summary_content>
